mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal a broad demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with an accuracy exceeding 55%, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial production growth, supply-side capacity constraints trigger inflationary expectations that prompt preemptive monetary tightening, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 5-day returns during overheating regimes, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real household purchasing power and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next three months.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or the correlation coefficient remains above -0.05 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth crosses below 1.5% annualized, weak aggregate demand triggers immediate equity repricing, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5%, invalidating the demand shock transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to generate positive 20-day returns with statistical significance, or the interaction effect reverses direction during labor market normalization.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate capacity and rising profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.
